summary:
The Bezold-Jarisch reflex results from a change in heart rate and blood pressure triggered primarily through baroreceptor stimulation after an IV stimulus; among potential mediators like histamine or others listed, its role as causing vascular effects makes it plausible despite lack of explicit proof here. Agent2 reasoned based on indirect mechanisms involving histamine’s known actions leading to cardiovascular changes consistent with described symptoms during the reflex event. 

Note: Summary has been condensed into two coherent statements while retaining core elements discussed above regarding mechanism & rationale behind selecting 'Histamine' option out all provided ones related clinically significant aspects pertinent to understanding Bezold Jarisch Reflex properly without adding extraneous details beyond scope required per instructions.
